title: Your team just moved from manual model deployment to a GitHub Actions pipeline. What stages does an ML CI/CD pipeline need that a normal software CI/CD pipeline doesn't?
prompt:
Your team just moved from manual model deployment to a GitHub Actions pipeline. What stages does an ML CI/CD pipeline need that a normal software CI/CD pipeline doesn't?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
A normal software pipeline mostly verifies code correctness: lint, unit test, build, deploy. An ML pipeline needs all of that plus stages that verify the data and resulting model are actually good, because the same code can produce a great model or a broken one depending entirely on the data it trains on. On top of the usual stages I'd add: a data validation stage checking schema, ranges, and missing values before anything expensive runs; a training stage producing a versioned model artifact; a model evaluation stage against a held-out or golden dataset; a comparison stage against the current production model; and only then a gradual deployment stage.

**Takeaway:** Software CI/CD assumes correctness is a property of code — ML correctness is a property of code plus data plus statistical behavior, which can only be evaluated by actually running the pipeline against real data.
hint_notes: Add to standard lint/test/build/deploy: data validation, versioned training artifact, held-out-set model evaluation, comparison against current prod model, then gradual deployment — because correctness depends on data, not just code.
